content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but the app uses CoreLocation to record GPS routes (App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-18) and the description advertises "maps every run with GPS" (description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple cares because the published privacy policy must accurately reflect actual data practices, and a direct contradiction is a common 5.1.1(i) rejection. Here the code collects and stores location fixes in `route` while the policy flatly denies collecting location, and the marketing copy touts GPS mapping. Update the policy to disclose location collection (and clarify on-device vs. iCloud storage), and confirm PrivacyInfo declares the location data type.
